subject: bio-link question for you, Yessica
body:
Hey Yessica,

I spent some time this morning going through your latest Houston market analysis. The level of competence you show is high-tier, but I was curious: Are you currently seeing a high "drop-off" rate when people click your link, or are the inquiries actually matching your view count?

I ask because I noticed your current link structure is accidentally designed for "Likers" (social validation) rather than "Buyers" (business results). It’s a subtle technical mismatch I see even the top 1% making.

If you’re interested, I’d be happy to record a quick screen-walkthrough showing you where that friction is and how it’s likely capping your inbound. I’m doing this to build more authority and case studies in the Houston market—no pitch or call for now. 

Open to a look?

Best,
Ryan

subject: the "inquiry leak" on your profile
body:
Hey Angela,

I genuinely admire the mindset and professionalism you bring to your Houston {{Industry}} work. Your audience clearly trusts your information.

However, from an conversion standpoint, your current profile setup is like having a high-end storefront where the door doesn't quite open. People watch, they trust you, but they "bounce" at the final step because the link doesn't match their intent. It’s likely costing you a handful of serious prospects every month.
